4 · Findings by Account

PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION strong
Account Number 18306700003 · IDFC First Bank · Score 1,082
Rank #1

The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) exhibits suspicious money behavior, characterized by a large credit transaction of 2000000.00 and multiple subsequent debit transactions. This pattern, combined with other findings, raises concerns about potential money laundering or structuring activities. The account's transactions demonstrate a high degree of complexity and potential manipulation.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit transaction of 2000000.00 was traced into 6 subsequent debit transactions, with the entire amount allocated and none remaining untraced. (+7 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — The account made 15 credit transactions on 2025-04-03, each below the individual cutoff, totaling 4958966.00. (+3 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — A possible extraction false positive was identified due to a duplicate verification pattern with a high confidence score but no corroborating match. (+9 more of this type)
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — A debit of 500000.0 was followed by a near-equal credit of 500000.0 with explicit reversal/reference evidence, 0 days apart.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 166 nodes and 347 directed transaction edges, indicating complex transaction activity.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — The account has 5 debit-reversal pairs, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 45 suspicious round-value debits totaling 13396000.00, with repeated amounts, such as 200,000 x16 and 300,000 x11.
Assessment: 8 strong pattern type(s) and 6 distinct pattern(s) — strong evidence.

Account Number 92883409730 · IDFC First Bank · Score 647

The graph shows 1338 transactions over a period from 2019-11-26 to 2025-10-03, with 12 transactions flagged and excluded. The graph shows various fraud patterns with the most notable being Duplicate Verification with 142 occurrences. The busiest pattern is Duplicate Verification, which stands out significantly from other patterns such as Failed Reversed Transaction Detection with 1 occurrence. An investigator should take note of the high frequency of Duplicate Verification and the relatively low counts of other patterns like Structuring Smurfing Detection with 9 occurrences and Money Trail Tracing with 18 occurrences.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
